title: Commercial pilot launch and operations review
expected_decisions: Limit the initial commercial pilot to twenty customer users.
Limit standard pilot recordings to thirty minutes.
Allow sixty-minute recordings only for approved evaluation accounts.
Use email as the primary pilot support channel.
Retain uploaded pilot recordings for thirty days.
Keep single sign-on outside the initial commercial scope.
Do not announce the launch date until pricing and security approval are complete.
Review pilot quality and usage after the first twenty processed meetings.

expected_actions.action: Circulate the approved pilot pricing table.
Complete the storage and access-control security review.
Confirm the first pilot customer participant list.
Run the twelve-recording regression suite and document failures.
Verify recording deletion from storage after the retention test.
Confirm whether regional data storage is required.
Review whether contractor accounts may join the pilot.

expected_risks: Delayed pricing approval could postpone commercial follow-up.
An incomplete security review could delay launch approval.
Poor-quality recordings could reduce transcription and action recall.
Unexpected sixty-minute usage could increase processing cost and delay.
Unclear retention requirements could delay privacy approval.
An unconfirmed customer user count could create inaccurate capacity assumptions.
expected_open_questions: Whether the customer requires single sign-on.
Whether recordings must remain in a specific geographic region.
Whether external contractors will participate in the pilot.
Whether sixty-minute support is essential for the first commercial customer.
rejected_proposals: Launch publicly without an invited pilot.
Include custom reporting in the standard pilot.
Promise single sign-on before requirements are confirmed.
must_not_invent: Owners for the two explicitly unassigned actions
Deadlines for actions that have no deadline
Additional actions introduced only in the final recap
audio_notes: Synthetic four-speaker controlled business meeting generated at 135 words per minute with natural calculated pauses.
